_meta.dna: recipe(economics) = discover(llm) x record(steps) x replay(deterministic) x cost(zero) x share(store) x version(immutable) x evidence(sealed)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions. Questions must be EXECUTABLE — answerable by checking if something actually works, not theoretical capability.

name: Tower of Recipe Economics
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 29, Berlin, DevOps engineer at a mid-size startup. Expert tech level. Uses Solace Browser daily. Wants to create recipes from her daily LinkedIn outreach workflow and share them with her team.
questions: When I perform a LinkedIn outreach sequence manually, does the browser offer to record my steps as a new recipe?
Can I name and tag the recipe during creation so I can find it later in my recipe library?
Does the recipe creation wizard show me each captured step with a screenshot so I can verify what was recorded?
Can I delete or reorder individual steps in the recipe before saving it?
Does the recipe capture the CSS selectors and page structure so it can replay on slightly different page layouts?
Can I add a description and usage notes to the recipe that other users will see before running it?
Does the system warn me if my recipe contains steps that require authentication tokens that will expire?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all recipe creation edge cases. Nadia is Expert level — she needs the recording to be reliable, editable, and shareable. Every answer must point to a working UI element or API endpoint.

floors.persona_context: 34, Austin, QA engineer at an e-commerce company. Expert tech level. Needs to record multi-step browser workflows that include form fills, clicks, waits, and file uploads for product listing automation.
questions: Does the step recorder capture form field inputs including dropdown selections and date pickers?
Can the recorder handle a workflow that spans multiple tabs — for example, copying data from a Google Sheet tab into a web form tab?
Does the recorder capture file upload actions so I can replay uploading product images?
Can I insert explicit wait steps for pages that load dynamically with AJAX content?
Does the recorder distinguish between typing in a search box and pressing Enter to submit?
Can I record a conditional branch — for example, 'if the price field is empty, skip this row'?
Does the recorder capture scroll actions needed to reach elements below the fold on long pages?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all step recording mechanics. Carlos needs robust recording that handles real-world web complexity — dynamic content, multi-tab, file uploads, conditionals. If recording fails on any of these, the recipe is useless.

floors.persona_context: 41, Seoul, Operations Manager at a logistics company. Intermediate tech level. Runs the same supplier outreach recipe 50 times per week. Needs identical output every time — no hallucination, no variation, no LLM re-generation.
questions: When I replay a recipe that was approved last week, does it produce exactly the same email text without calling the LLM again?
Can I see a diff between the original recipe output and today's replay to confirm they are identical?
Does replay skip the LLM entirely and use the sealed output from the preview step?
If the target website changed its layout since the recipe was recorded, does replay fail loudly instead of silently producing wrong output?
Can I replay the same recipe on 50 different supplier contacts in a batch without approving each one individually?
Does the replay execution time show that no LLM API call was made (zero LLM latency in the evidence log)?
If I replay a recipe offline (no internet), does it still work for the deterministic steps that don't need network access?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all deterministic replay scenarios. Jenny runs 50 replays per week — any variation or LLM re-call is a cost leak and a trust violation. Replay must be provably identical to the approved output.

floors.persona_context: 36, Lagos, Startup founder bootstrapping with a $200/month total tool budget. BYOK with his own Anthropic key. Needs to see exactly how much each recipe costs on first run vs replay, and project monthly spend before committing.
questions: Can I see the exact LLM token cost of the first run (preview) for a recipe before I approve it?
Does the cost dashboard show me that replays cost $0 in LLM tokens with a clear comparison to first-run cost?
Can I set a monthly budget cap in dollars so the system blocks new recipe previews when I hit the limit?
Does the usage page show my cost curve — proving that cost per task decreases as I replay more recipes?
Can I see a projected monthly cost based on my current usage pattern before the month ends?
Does the system show me which recipes are the most expensive and suggest converting them to replay-only?
Can I export my full cost breakdown as a CSV to reconcile with my Anthropic API billing?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all cost tracking and economics scenarios. Trevor has $200/month total — every dollar matters. The cost dashboard must prove the recipe economics thesis: first run costs money, replays are free, cost curve inverts.

floors.persona_context: 27, Osaka, Freelance social media manager with 8 clients. Intermediate tech level. Wants to share her best Instagram posting recipe with other freelancers through the Stillwater Store, and discover recipes others have published.
questions: Can I publish my Instagram posting recipe to the Stillwater Store with a description, tags, and preview screenshots?
Does the store show me how many people have installed my recipe and their average success rate?
Can I browse the store and filter recipes by category (social media, email, CRM) and sort by popularity?
Does the store verify that a recipe actually works before listing it (automated testing or review)?
Can I install someone else's recipe and customize it for my clients without modifying the original?
Does the store show the cost-per-run for each recipe so I can compare before installing?
Can I share a recipe directly with a specific person via a link without publishing it to the entire store?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all recipe sharing and store workflows. Suki wants a two-sided marketplace — she publishes and she discovers. The store must show real metrics (installs, success rate, cost) not just descriptions.

floors.persona_context: 52, Vienna, Compliance officer at a pharmaceutical company. Expert tech level. Needs immutable recipe versions for FDA Part 11 compliance — every change must be tracked, no silent modifications, full audit trail of who changed what and when.
questions: Does every recipe save create a new immutable version with a unique hash, not overwrite the previous version?
Can I view the complete version history of a recipe showing all changes, timestamps, and the author of each change?
Does the system prevent me from modifying a recipe version that has already been executed in production?
Can I roll back to a previous recipe version and re-run it to reproduce an exact historical result?
Does the version diff show me exactly which steps changed between version 3 and version 7 of a recipe?
Is each recipe version signed with a SHA-256 hash that I can independently verify has not been tampered with?
Can I lock a recipe version so that no one on my team can create a new version without compliance approval?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all version management and immutability scenarios. Martin works in pharma — FDA Part 11 requires every modification to be traceable, immutable, and auditable. Silent changes are a regulatory violation.

floors.persona_context: 39, Mumbai, Legal operations manager at a law firm. Intermediate tech level. Needs sealed evidence that a recipe was executed exactly as recorded — for client billing, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting.
questions: After a recipe executes, can I see a sealed evidence bundle with timestamps, screenshots, and the exact steps that ran?
Does the evidence bundle include a SHA-256 hash chain that proves no step was added or removed after execution?
Can I export the evidence bundle as a PDF or ZIP that I can attach to a client invoice as proof of work?
Does the sealed evidence include the recipe version hash so I can prove which exact recipe version was executed?
Can I search my evidence history by date range, recipe name, or client to find a specific execution record?
Does the evidence seal include a timestamp from a trusted source (not just my local clock)?
If someone tampers with the evidence bundle after export, does the hash chain detect the modification when I verify it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all evidence sealing scenarios. Rina needs legally defensible proof that a recipe ran exactly as recorded. Evidence bundles must be exportable, searchable, tamper-evident, and tied to specific recipe versions.
